message:
perf(http/h1): use memchr for header scanning and add request-line fast path

Replace manual window-scan loops with SIMD-accelerated alternatives:

- find_headers_end() uses memmem::find for \r\n\r\n detection instead of
  .windows(4).position(), leveraging Boyer-Moore-Horspool / SIMD in memchr
- find_crlf() uses memchr(b'\n') with a preceding-\r check
- parse_request_line_bytes() gains a two-memchr fast path for the common
  METHOD SP URI SP VERSION wire form, falling back to the tolerant
  tokenizer (now parse_request_line_bytes_slow) only for malformed input
- decode_head() defers the request-line CRLF scan to the incomplete-buffer
  case, avoiding an extra scan on the fully-buffered decode hot path
- Add memchr = "2.8" dependency to Cargo.toml

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
